message.content:
PLAYER: "Scrodinger" (multiple accounts).
RATINGS: chess.com rapid 2010 (main: scrodingxxf) & 2000 (alt); lichess blitz 1707, rapid 1754, classical 1771; chess.com blitz best 1612, currently lower; bullet ~1150.
TACTICS/PUZZLES: chess.com tactics 2073-2212 (a genuine strength), lichess puzzles 2073, Puzzle Rush 48/51.
ESTIMATED FIDE OTB: ~1600-1700. Has NOT played FIDE-rated OTB yet.
REPERTOIRE — White: 1.e4 player. vs 1...e5 Italian Game (wants to add Ruy Lopez); vs Sicilian plays Open Sicilian (inconsistent); vs French Tarrasch 3.Nd2; vs Caro-Kann Advance 3.e5.
REPERTOIRE — Black: vs 1.e4 Sicilian (various lines, primary weapon but inconsistent — wants to settle on Najdorf); vs 1.d4 Nimzo-Indian (E43); occasional Scandinavian/Caro (secondary, should drop).
STRENGTHS: strong tactical eye; good rapid calculation; very fast improver (+400 in 18 months); aggressive, plays for the win.
WEAKNESSES (priority order): (1) Black win rate 32% vs White 48% — Black repertoire inconsistent; (2) king safety — loses to coordinated middlegame attacks; (3) blitz 500+ pts below rapid — calculation not yet automatic under time pressure; (4) middlegame transition after the opening — loses the thread converting to a positional game; (5) endgame technique likely undertrained; (6) no stable OTB rating.
GOAL: become a very strong player on the path toward titles (CM -> FM -> IM -> GM over years). Wants a personalized course/resource curriculum.


YOU ARE AN ADVERSARIAL FACT-CHECKER for chess resources. Category: "Master Game Collections & Classics".

Here are researched resources as JSON:
[
 {
  "name": "The Life and Games of Mikhail Tal",
  "rtype": "Book",
  "provider": "Mikhail Tal (Everyman Chess)",
  "url": "https://everymanchess.com/products/the-life-and-games-of-mikhail-tal",
  "cost": "$29.95 (USD), ~496 pp",
  "level": "1700-2300",
  "why": "Scrodinger is an aggressive, plays-for-the-win attacker whose strength is a tactical eye and rapid calculation. Tal is the patron saint of intuitive sacrificial attack, and his self-annotations teach how to manufacture the coordinated middlegame attacks that Scrodinger currently SUFFERS from (king-safety losses, weakness #2) by showing them from the attacker's side. Directly feeds his open-Sicilian and Najdorf ambitions where dynamic sacrifice is the whole point.",
  "priority": "Essential",
  "time": "1 book ~3 months"
 },
 {
  "name": "Tal-Botvinnik 1960: Match for the World Chess Championship",
  "rtype": "Book",
  "provider": "Mikhail Tal (Russell Enterprises)",
  "url": "https://www.russell-enterprises.com/russell-enterprises/tal-botvinnik-1960",
  "cost": "$19.95 softcover / $11.99 ebook (USD), 192 pp",
  "level": "1700-2200",
  "why": "All 21 games annotated by Tal himself in his most creative period — a focused, finishable model of attacking chess that suits Scrodinger's aggressive style far better than a sprawling anthology. The match is a clinic in seizing the initiative and exploiting an opponent's loose king, exactly the skill Scrodinger needs to convert his tactical strength while plugging his own king-safety leak (weakness #2).",
  "priority": "Essential",
  "time": "20-30 hrs"
 },
 {
  "name": "Zurich International Chess Tournament, 1953",
  "rtype": "Book",
  "provider": "David Bronstein (Dover Publications)",
  "url": "https://store.doverpublications.com/products/9780486238005",
  "cost": "~$16 (USD), 384 pp",
  "level": "1900-2400",
  "why": "The single best book for the exact gap in Scrodinger's game: the middlegame transition after the opening (weakness #4 — 'loses the thread converting to a positional game'). Bronstein narrates the WHY of plans and pawn structures, and the tournament is saturated with Nimzo-Indian and King's-Indian battles that map onto his Black 1.d4 repertoire (Nimzo E43). Pushes him from tactical executor toward positional understanding needed for the 2200+ climb.",
  "priority": "Essential",
  "time": "1 book ~3 months"
 },
 {
  "name": "Logical Chess: Move by Move (Every Move Explained, Algebraic Edition)",
  "rtype": "Book",
  "provider": "Irving Chernev (Batsford)",
  "url": "https://www.batsfordbooks.com/book/logical-chess-move-by-move-2/",
  "cost": "$24.95 (USD), ~256 pp",
  "level": "1600-1900",
  "why": "Every single move is justified in plain language — the fastest fix for weakness #4 (losing the thread after the opening) and the middlegame-transition problem that holds back his Black results (32% vs 48% as White, weakness #1). At his level it is a quick foundational read that makes positional logic automatic, freeing his strong calculation for when it actually matters.",
  "priority": "Recommended",
  "time": "15-25 hrs"
 },
 {
  "name": "The Most Instructive Games of Chess Ever Played: 62 Masterpieces of Chess Strategy",
  "rtype": "Book",
  "provider": "Irving Chernev (Batsford / Dover)",
  "url": "https://www.batsfordbooks.com/book/the-most-instructive-games-of-chess-ever-played/",
  "cost": "$23.95 Batsford / ~$13 Dover (USD), 320 pp",
  "level": "1700-2100",
  "why": "Each of the 62 games isolates ONE strategic theme and resolves it cleanly — ideal for Scrodinger's documented difficulty converting a won/equal opening into a coherent plan (weakness #4) and for raising his sub-par Black conversion rate (weakness #1). Builds the positional scaffolding a tactician needs to stop drifting once the forcing lines run out.",
  "priority": "Recommended",
  "time": "20-30 hrs"
 },
 {
  "name": "My 60 Memorable Games",
  "rtype": "Book",
  "provider": "Bobby Fischer (Batsford)",
  "url": "https://www.amazon.com/My-Memorable-Games-Bobby-Fischer/dp/190638830X",
  "cost": "~$20-25 (USD), 384 pp",
  "level": "1900-2400",
  "why": "Fischer was THE great 1.e4 Sicilian theoretician on both sides of the board — directly relevant to Scrodinger's White Open Sicilian and his goal of settling on the Najdorf as Black. The brutally honest, deeply concrete annotations model the precise calculation-under-structure that a fast-rising tactician (his +400/18mo strength) must internalize to break into master strength on the CM->FM path.",
  "priority": "Recommended",
  "time": "1 book ~3 months"
 },
 {
  "name": "Understanding Chess Move by Move",
  "rtype": "Book",
  "provider": "John Nunn (Gambit Publications)",
  "url": "http://www.gambitbooks.com/books/Understanding_Chess_Move_by_Move.html",
  "cost": "~$24.95 list / ~$18 new (USD), 240 pp",
  "level": "1800-2200",
  "why": "Nunn's 30 modern games are chosen and explained specifically to teach the practical decision-making and king-safety judgment Scrodinger lacks (weakness #2). Sits exactly between Chernev and Bronstein in difficulty, making it the right bridge for a ~2000-rapid/2073-puzzle player who calculates well but needs verbalized middlegame understanding to make calculation automatic (also helps weakness #3, blitz).",
  "priority": "Recommended",
  "time": "20-30 hrs"
 },
 {
  "name": "Chess: The Art of Logical Thinking — From the First Move to the Last",
  "rtype": "Book",
  "provider": "Neil McDonald (Batsford)",
  "url": "https://www.amazon.com/Chess-Logical-Thinking-First-Move/dp/0713488948",
  "cost": "~$20 (USD), 208 pp",
  "level": "1700-2000",
  "why": "30 modern (1978-2002) games with a comment on EVERY move, many of them sharp Sicilian and Indian fights — a near-perfect difficulty match for Scrodinger right now and a smoother step up from Logical Chess. Each game is chosen for a single logical thread carried to the end, training the after-the-opening planning he loses (weakness #4) in openings he actually plays.",
  "priority": "Recommended",
  "time": "15-25 hrs"
 },
 {
  "name": "My Great Predecessors (Part I-V)",
  "rtype": "Book",
  "provider": "Garry Kasparov (Everyman Chess / New In Chess reissue)",
  "url": "https://www.newinchess.com/my-great-predecessors-collection-garry-kasparov-part-1-5",
  "cost": "~$30-35 per volume (USD)",
  "level": "2000-2500",
  "why": "The deepest survey of how chess (and especially attacking and Sicilian theory) evolved — long-term study fuel for Scrodinger's explicit multi-year CM->FM->IM->GM goal. Volume II (Botvinnik, Smyslov, Tal) and the Fischer volume reinforce his Sicilian repertoire and the dynamic-imbalance play that fits his aggressive identity. Buy one volume at a time as he climbs past 2100.",
  "priority": "Optional",
  "time": "ongoing, 1 volume ~4-6 months"
 },
 {
  "name": "Garry Kasparov on Modern Chess (Revolution in the 70s + Kasparov vs Karpov series)",
  "rtype": "Book",
  "provider": "Garry Kasparov (Everyman Chess)",
  "url": "https://www.chess.com/forum/view/chess-equipment/garry-kasparov-on-modern-chess-and-other-kasparov-annotated-game-books",
  "cost": "~$30-40 per volume (USD)",
  "level": "2100-2500",
  "why": "Explicitly covers cutting-edge Sicilian, Nimzo-Indian and other lines that ARE Scrodinger's repertoire (Najdorf ambitions, Nimzo E43), through the lens of the most prepared attacker in history. Aspirational, repertoire-relevant deep study for the back half of his title journey — flagged Optional because it is overkill until he is consistently above ~2100 and has settled his Black openings (weakness #1).",
  "priority": "Optional",
  "time": "ongoing, advanced"
 }
]

For EACH resource: use WebSearch/WebFetch to confirm it genuinely exists, the author/provider is correct, it is currently available, and the price is roughly right. Correct any wrong URL/price/author/level. Set verified=true ONLY if you confirmed it real and accurately described; otherwise verified=false with a note. Default to skepticism: if you cannot confirm a specific course/book really exists, mark verified=false and confidence=low. Drop obvious hallucinations entirely. Also re-check that each "why" is genuinely personalized to Scrodinger and tighten it if weak. Reputable, well-known resources (Silman, Dvoretsky, Vukovic, Naroditsky, Saint Louis CC, established Chessable bestsellers) can be high-confidence. Return the cleaned list.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- contribute:contribute: This skill should be used when the user asks to "contribute to open source", "find an issue to work on", "analyze a repo", "submit a pull request", "review PR feedback", "triage an issue", "sync my fork", "create a release", "clean up contribution branches", "debug CI failures", or mentions open-source contribution workflows. Provides a full-lifecycle open-source contribution system with 12 phases.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: You've hit your session limit · resets 1:50am (Asia/Calcutta)
